title: feat(enter): pre-fill localhost redirect URI for new app keys
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Seeds `http://localhost/callback` into the app-key creation dialog (simplified/app-key mode only)
- Removes the friction of manually registering a localhost redirect just to develop locally
- Keeps the per-app allowlist gate intact (no blanket localhost allow) — avoids the open-redirect/token-theft class that "allow any URL + warn" would open
- Loopback ports are already wildcarded (RFC 8252 §7.3), so only the path needs editing for most dev servers
- Adds a dev note in the editor: edit the path to match your dev server, remove before production

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Seed the localhost
gist.blurb: Creating a new app key now pre-fills a localhost redirect URI so you can start building immediately. Less typing, more coding, and still perfectly secure.
gist.summary: The app-key creation dialog now pre-fills `http://localhost/callback` as a redirect URI to reduce friction for local development.
gist.impact: Developers can immediately test OAuth flows locally without manually registering a localhost redirect, while maintaining strict per-app allowlist security.
gist.visual_concept: The pollinations bee planting a glowing seed (representing the pre-filled URI) into a small retro server box or digital garden pot labeled 'localhost'.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot planting a glowing green seed into a small retro server box with a screen showing 'localhost'.
